description: Composable DSP, UI, and analysis primitives for agentically assembling plugin-suite products without collapsing back to slider-first manifests.

researchSources.title: Digital Dynamic Range Compressor Design - A Tutorial and Analysis
Reducing the Aliasing of Nonlinear Waveshaping Using Continuous-Time Convolution
Virtual Analog Modeling of Audio Circuitry Using Wave Digital Filters

equalization.title: Equalization
equalization.auditFinding: EQ should be represented as a band topology plus direct graph manipulation, not as unrelated frequency/gain/Q sliders.
sophisticationPath: Start with stable RBJ-style biquad bands and clear control roles.
Promote bands into graph entities with filter kind, gain, frequency, Q, slope, and bypass as one editable unit.
Add dynamic/spectral behavior by attaching sidechain detectors and analyzer probes to individual bands.
Validate with magnitude-response sweeps, phase/latency expectations, and band-handle UI parity.

compression.title: Compression
compression.auditFinding: Dynamics plugins need explicit detector, gain-computer, ballistics, transfer-curve, and metering primitives so style changes are controlled design choices.
sophisticationPath: Start with a feed-forward topology, threshold/ratio/knee, and attack/release smoothing.
Model sidechain filtering, detector domain, and timing as separate agent-visible choices.
Expose transfer curves and gain-history surfaces as primary editors instead of hiding behavior behind slider banks.
Validate with step response, tone-burst recovery, crest-factor material, and gain-reduction trace assertions.

saturation.title: Saturation
saturation.auditFinding: Saturation needs a declared nonlinearity, anti-aliasing strategy, tone-shaping context, and harmonic-analysis harness before it can be agentically tuned.
sophisticationPath: Start with a bounded memoryless shaper and gain staging.
Add oversampling or antiderivative-style anti-aliasing requirements when generated harmonics can exceed Nyquist.
Upgrade character models with multiband routing, pre/post EQ, feedback context, or wave-digital virtual-analog stages.
Validate with harmonic spectra, alias energy, loudness-normalized nulls, and intermodulation probes.

space.title: Space and Reverb
space.auditFinding: Reverb products need macro room geometry, decay contour, damping, and post-shaping primitives that can be staged from impulse and decay probes.
sophisticationPath: Start with room size, decay, damping, and mix as a macro field.
Add decay-rate EQ and post-EQ as direct visual surfaces.
Validate with impulse response, decay slope, stereo width, and freeze/gate probes.

delay.title: Delay
delay.auditFinding: Delay products need tap timing, feedback topology, tone shaping, and modulation primitives rather than disconnected time/mix sliders.
sophisticationPath: Start with tempo-aware taps and feedback gain.
Add feedback-path filtering, diffusion, and modulation slots.
Validate with impulse repeats, feedback stability, and modulation sideband probes.

instrument.title: Instrument Voice
instrument.auditFinding: Synth products need oscillator, filter, envelope, performance, and modulation primitives that expose voice architecture directly.
sophisticationPath: Start with oscillator, filter, envelope, amplifier, and output primitives.
Add modulation routing, voice allocation, and performance surfaces.
Validate with note-on envelopes, tuning, aliasing, and modulation-depth probes.

eq.parametric-band.title: Parametric Band
eq.parametric-band.description: A cohesive EQ band entity with filter type, frequency, gain, Q/bandwidth, slope, and bypass represented together.
eq.parametric-band.dspIntent: Use stable filter coefficients or Faust filter primitives while preserving boost/cut reciprocity and predictable bandwidth mapping.
agentDesignNotes: Bind frequency/gain/Q as one surface-owned band before adding fallback dock controls.
Prefer typed band descriptors over independent slider sections.

eq.dynamic-band.title: Dynamic or Spectral Band
eq.dynamic-band.description: An EQ band whose gain or selectivity responds to detector energy in a focused spectral region.
eq.dynamic-band.dspIntent: Attach sidechain detection and bounded gain movement to a filter band while keeping static EQ response inspectable.
agentDesignNotes: Represent static and dynamic curves separately so agents can compare what the band is versus what it is doing.
Require a detector probe before claiming spectral or adaptive behavior.

eq.filterbank-crossover.title: Filterbank or Crossover
eq.filterbank-crossover.description: A split/recombine structure for multiband EQ, dynamics, and saturation products.
eq.filterbank-crossover.dspIntent: Use complementary band-splits or clearly documented crossover behavior so band routing remains phase-aware.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive whenever a product claims multiband behavior.
Do not model split frequencies as generic sliders when the region editor can own them.

compression.feedforward-sidechain.title: Feed-Forward Sidechain
compression.feedforward-sidechain.description: A predictable dynamics topology with a sidechain level detector driving a gain computer and smoothing stage.
compression.feedforward-sidechain.dspIntent: Separate detector, gain computer, smoothing, makeup, and gain stage so topology/style choices stay inspectable.
agentDesignNotes: Treat compression style as a topology preset over detector and ballistics, not a cosmetic enum.
Always pair the transfer curve with a time-domain gain-reduction trace.

compression.detector-ballistics.title: Detector Ballistics
compression.detector-ballistics.description: Peak/RMS/log-domain detection plus attack, hold, release, lookahead, and linking rules.
compression.detector-ballistics.dspIntent: Make memory and smoothing explicit because dynamics behavior is nonlinear and time-dependent.
agentDesignNotes: Surface sidechain filters visually; they are part of the compressor's sound, not secondary settings.
Use meter history to explain ballistics rather than relying on numeric attack/release controls.

compression.multiband-dynamics.title: Multiband Dynamics Region
compression.multiband-dynamics.description: A crossover-owned region that applies compression, expansion, or gating behavior within a frequency band.
compression.multiband-dynamics.dspIntent: Compose filterbank crossover, detector ballistics, and gain computer primitives per band.
agentDesignNotes: Band regions should own crossover and threshold gestures together.
Expose compression versus expansion mode as a region behavior, not a separate product concept.

saturation.memoryless-waveshaper.title: Memoryless Waveshaper
saturation.memoryless-waveshaper.description: A static nonlinear transfer function with explicit drive, bias, symmetry, and output compensation.
saturation.memoryless-waveshaper.dspIntent: Apply bounded nonlinear mapping with visible transfer curve and gain staging.
agentDesignNotes: A drive slider without a declared curve and output strategy is not enough to describe saturation.
Pair every nonlinear transfer with harmonic analysis probes.

saturation.antialiasing-strategy.title: Anti-Aliasing Strategy
saturation.antialiasing-strategy.description: Oversampling, continuous-time convolution, or antiderivative-style mitigation for nonlinear stages.
saturation.antialiasing-strategy.dspIntent: Constrain alias energy generated by nonlinear processing and declare the cost/quality tradeoff.
agentDesignNotes: Any product with aggressive drive should carry an anti-aliasing primitive.
Expose quality/latency explicitly so generated plugins can be staged and compared.

saturation.virtual-analog-stage.title: Virtual-Analog Stage
saturation.virtual-analog-stage.description: A circuit-inspired nonlinear block whose topology, feedback context, and component roles are explicit.
saturation.virtual-analog-stage.dspIntent: Use wave-digital, zero-delay-feedback, or carefully bounded circuit-inspired models when character claims exceed static waveshaping.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive when the app claims analog character, tube/diode behavior, or nonlinear filter feedback.
Require stability and aliasing probes before making the stage a flagship feature.

saturation.multiband-drive.title: Multiband Drive
saturation.multiband-drive.description: A band-split saturation workstation with per-band style, drive, tone, dynamics, and modulation hooks.
saturation.multiband-drive.dspIntent: Compose crossover and saturation stages so each band can be staged, metered, and auditioned independently.
agentDesignNotes: Use the multiband editor as the primary control surface, with fallback controls only for non-gestural details.
Treat modulation targets as part of the primitive so agents can wire movement consistently.

space.reverb-macro-field.title: Reverb Macro Field
space.reverb-macro-field.description: A room-shaping primitive that groups size, decay, damping, brightness, width, and freeze/gate behavior as one spatial editor.
space.reverb-macro-field.dspIntent: Expose perceptual room controls while keeping decay contour and post-shaping inspectable.
agentDesignNotes: Represent room shape as a field of linked nodes rather than isolated room-size and decay sliders.
Pair every reverb macro with impulse-response inspection.

delay.tap-feedback-network.title: Tap and Feedback Network
delay.tap-feedback-network.description: A tempo-aware delay primitive with tap placement, feedback routing, filtering, diffusion, and modulation slots.
delay.tap-feedback-network.dspIntent: Make time, feedback, filter, and modulation behavior visible as one repeat network.
agentDesignNotes: Use a timeline as the primary editor for delay time and tap relationships.
Treat feedback filtering and modulation as part of the delay network, not separate utility sections.

instrument.oscillator-filter-voice.title: Oscillator Filter Voice
instrument.oscillator-filter-voice.description: A subtractive voice primitive that binds oscillator color, filter shaping, envelope timing, performance, and output behavior.
instrument.oscillator-filter-voice.dspIntent: Expose the playable voice path from oscillator through filter and amplitude envelope with visible modulation hooks.
agentDesignNotes: Represent the synth as a voice path and modulation system, not a generic parameter grid.
Keep performance state visible so generated instruments can be staged interactively.
